mechanism_narrative: High and accelerating inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes real corporate earnings and prompts tighter monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth was approximately 8% in April 2022
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite high inflation, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL growth above 5% and forward returns is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained job growth, indicated by PAYEMS, reflects a healthy and expanding economy, which supports consumer spending and corporate profits, translating to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS showed strong growth in April 2022
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth consistently leads to negative 60d S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window, or the positive correlation becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A significant contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity and a broader economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 shows positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO growth falls below -2%, or the signal's predictive power is statistically insignificant over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales growth (RSAFS) reflects strong consumer demand and confidence, which directly boosts corporate revenues and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth consistently leads to negative 5d S&P 500 returns over any 2-year rolling window, or the positive correlation becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve is actively raising interest rates (FEDFUNDS increasing) while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is low, it signals an aggressive tightening cycle aimed at curbing inflation, which typically leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon due to higher discount rates and recession fears.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed began raising rates in March 2022 and UNRATE was 3.6% in April 2022
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with low unemployment
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between FEDFUNDS change and UNRATE level does not significantly predict negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts (HOUST) indicates a slowdown in the construction sector and broader economic activity, which can spill over into consumer confidence and lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average declines by > 10% from its peak
failure_condition: S&P 500 shows positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST declines by more than 10%, or the signal's predictive power is statistically insignificant over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Following a period of negative GDP growth, a return to positive growth (GDPC1) signals an economic recovery, which typically leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as corporate earnings improve.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth turns positive after at least one quarter of negative growth
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances where GDPC1 growth turns positive after a contraction, or the signal's predictive power is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Extremely low unemployment (UNRATE) combined with persistent high inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates a tight labor market contributing to wage-price spiral concerns, forcing the Fed to maintain a hawkish stance, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 3.6% and CPIAUCSL YoY growth was ~8% in April 2022
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction between low unemployment and high inflation does not significantly predict negative returns.
